target_window_or_role: post-2012 PRN-free aP plus historical comparison
opportunity_status: highest-value historical read-first backlog after direct contemporary intake
absent_from_current_manifest: substantial residual headroom across five paired-Illumina WGS studies
recommended_intake_route: prioritize historical paired-Illumina read-first intake before new sensitivity analysis
notes: Japan has already absorbed the highest-yield contemporary prefecture batches, but the largest remaining public expansion path is still historical: one 1982-2014 cohort, one broad NIID series spanning 2000-2025, and residual 2024-2025 prefecture-level material remain outside the current manifest.

study_title: Bordetella pertussis population dynamics and phylogeny in Japan after adoption of acellular pertussis vaccines
notes: Best historical bridge for pre-2012 Japan and bounded support around PRN-free rollout.
Largest single Japanese longitudinal opportunity remaining outside the current manifest.

target_window_or_role: post-2012 PRN-free aP contemporary prefecture window

notes: Twelve Shimane genomes were already integrated here; the residual runs remain useful for recent PRN-free-era Japan replication.

target_window_or_role: transition-era whole-cell to PRN-exposed aP comparison
opportunity_status: residual split intake with isolate-WGS main branch and culture-independent sidecars
absent_from_current_manifest: residual isolate-WGS cohorts plus newer mixed culture-independent projects
recommended_intake_route: retain isolate WGS as the main branch; keep capture and metagenomic studies as sidecars
notes: Australia has already absorbed the highest-yield transition-era isolate-WGS runs targeted in this revision, but additional isolate-WGS material and newer targeted-capture, metagenomic-WGS, and amplicon studies can still strengthen the transition narrative without being forced into the main denominator.
Five Australia 2008-2012 genomes were already integrated here; the remainder is still the best public isolate-WGS bridge for the early transition period.

study_title: Molecular epidemiology of Bordetella pertussis, 2013 to 2017, Australia
notes: Five Australia 2013-2017 genomes were already integrated here; the remainder still extends the comparison beyond the early switch period with straightforward paired-Illumina isolate WGS.

target_window_or_role: recent direct-from-specimen and mixed isolate follow-up
opportunity_status: sidecar candidate with partial isolate-WGS component
study_title: Bordetella pertussis Genome sequencing and assembly
recommended_intake_route: split isolate-WGS rows into read-first intake and retain targeted-capture rows as a direct-from-specimen sidecar
notes: Contains 44 genomic-WGS runs plus 148 targeted-capture runs across 154 samples; useful, but not a single-caller denominator.

study_title: Culture independent multiplex amplicon and metagenomic sequencing of B. pertussis
recommended_intake_route: retain as a culture-independent sidecar rather than merge into isolate-defined denominators
notes: The study mixes 67 metagenomic-WGS runs with 306 amplicon runs; useful for opportunity mapping, not direct denominator replacement.

opportunity_status: partially realized formulation-clean read-first side branch
absent_from_current_manifest: residual Europe-cohort and historical Finland material remains outside the current manifest
recommended_intake_route: continue as a formulation-clean read-first side branch
notes: Finland is no longer empty after direct intake, but it still offers a compact expansion path with cleaner product-context interpretation than many larger countries.

study_title: Whole genome sequences of Bordetella isolates collected in Europe
notes: Six Finland genomes from the Europe 2014-2016 collection were already integrated here; the remaining sampled genomes remain outside the current manifest.
Three historical Finland genomes were already integrated here; the remaining historical series complements the Europe 2014-2016 cohort.

opportunity_status: late-era read-first branch now integrated; remaining limitation is the missing pre-switch comparator
absent_from_current_manifest: 0 for the Guangzhou and Tianjin late-era batches listed below after direct read-first augmentation
recommended_intake_route: retain as late-era context and prioritize any future pre-2012 comparator material
notes: China is not archive-poor: the current manifest retains 856 Chinese records, with 153 screen-relevant genomes PRN-interpretable in the selected-country screen. The informative rows still fall in the post-switch mixed-aP interval, so the Guangzhou and Tianjin read-first additions deepen that late-era branch but do not create the missing early comparison axis.

study_title: Raw sequence reads of 62 Bordetella pertussis isolates in Guangdong, China
recommended_intake_route: already incorporated through the read-first assembly path; retain as late China context
notes: Fully paired Illumina isolate WGS now incorporated through the direct public-read augmentation path; it strengthens the late China mixed-aP and macrolide-resistance context without adding an early comparator.

study_title: Whole genome sequencing of Bordetella pertussis in Tianjin, China
recommended_intake_route: already incorporated through the paired short-read assembly path; retain as late China context
notes: Clean paired DNBSEQ genomic WGS cohort now incorporated through the same short-read augmentation path as Guangzhou; it has direct value for the 2023 China window but remains a late-era side branch.

target_window_or_role: epidemiologic primary-set country with modest mechanistic headroom

notes: Great Britain remains estimable without new intake, but PRJEB45681 provides clean additional WGS coverage and an RNA-seq study should stay excluded.

study_title: Whole genome sequences of Bordetella isolates collected in Europe
notes: The Britain subset in PRJEB45681 is entirely absent from the current manifest and can sharpen the bounded primary-set contrast.

notes: Included here only to document explicit exclusion; all runs are transcriptomic RNA-seq rather than genomic WGS.
